rookie.title: Paradise Lost
rookie.flavor: The luxury resort promised total escape from the outside world. For one guest, that escape became permanent when they were found dead in their overwater bungalow.
rookie.emptyState: Paradise has a dark side tonight.

items.name: Marcus Chen
items.detail: Resort manager obsessed with maintaining perfect reputation.

items.name: Sophia Rivera
items.detail: Wealthy socialite with gambling debts and desperate needs.

items.name: Dr. James Thorne
items.detail: Resort physician who treated the victim recently.

items.name: Elena Vasquez
items.detail: Head bartender who served the victim's final drinks.

items.name: Tropical Plant Toxin
items.detail: Deadly oleander extract mixed into a cocktail.

items.name: Diving Weight
items.detail: Heavy metal weight used for underwater activities.

items.name: Kitchen Knife
items.detail: Sharp blade from the resort's main kitchen.

items.name: Prescription Pills
items.detail: Powerful sedatives from the medical clinic.

items.name: Overwater Bungalow
items.detail: Victim's private accommodation over the lagoon.

items.name: Beach Bar
items.detail: Open-air bar where final drinks were served.

items.name: Medical Clinic
items.detail: Small facility for treating guest injuries and illnesses.

items.name: Diving Equipment Shed
items.detail: Storage area for water sports gear and weights.

clues.heading: Toxic Cocktail Glass
clues.text: Lab analysis reveals oleander toxin residue in the victim's final drink glass. The poison causes cardiac arrest within hours of consumption.
clues.alone: The victim was killed by plant poison in their drink.

clues.heading: Bartender's Testimony
clues.text: Elena confirms she made the victim's usual rum punch at 9 PM. She used fresh tropical garnishes including oleander flowers for decoration.
clues.alone: Elena accidentally used poisonous flowers as drink garnish.

clues.heading: Security Camera Footage
clues.text: Video shows Sophia visiting the beach bar at 8:45 PM, fifteen minutes before the victim arrived. She appeared nervous and agitated.
clues.alone: Sophia was at the bar shortly before the victim.

clues.heading: Toxicology Report
clues.text: Victim's blood contains lethal levels of oleander toxin consistent with ingestion around 9:15 PM. Death occurred approximately 11:30 PM from cardiac arrest.
clues.alone: Poison was consumed around 9:15 PM at the bar.

links.insight: The toxic cocktail glass and blood analysis confirm oleander poisoning.

clues.heading: Guest Statement
clues.text: A guest saw Elena crying behind the bar after closing, saying she 'never meant for this to happen.' She was disposing of oleander flowers.
clues.alone: Elena was distraught about the accidental poisoning she caused.

links.insight: Elena's testimony and later remorse show she accidentally poisoned the victim.

clues.heading: Financial Records
clues.text: Sophia's credit cards were maxed out and she owed significant gambling debts. However, she left the island before 9 PM on the evening ferry.
clues.alone: Despite her financial troubles, Sophia had already departed the island.

links.insight: Camera footage was from the previous evening, not the night of the murder.

rookie.epilogue: Elena Vasquez, dedicated to creating beautiful tropical drinks, had unknowingly used deadly oleander flowers as garnish in the victim's cocktail. Her obsession with perfect presentation led to a tragic accident that cost a life. Despite her genuine remorse, her negligence proved fatal. She was charged with involuntary manslaughter, forever haunted by her deadly mistake.

clues.heading: Toxic Cocktail Glass
clues.text: Lab analysis reveals oleander toxin residue in the victim's final drink glass. The poison causes cardiac arrest within hours of consumption.
clues.alone: The victim was killed by plant poison in their drink.

clues.heading: Security Guard Report
clues.text: Guard captain states Elena was seen disposing of oleander flowers after the victim died, claiming they were 'accidentally used as garnish.'
clues.alone: Elena accidentally poisoned the victim with decorative flowers.

clues.heading: Text Messages
clues.text: Victim's phone shows threatening messages from Marcus: 'You'll destroy everything I've built here. I won't let that happen.'
clues.alone: Marcus had sent threatening messages to the victim.

clues.heading: Toxicology Report
clues.text: Victim's blood contains concentrated oleander toxin far exceeding accidental exposure levels. The dosage indicates deliberate poisoning, not decorative contamination.
clues.alone: The poison level proves intentional murder, not an accident.

links.insight: The toxic cocktail glass and blood analysis confirm deliberate oleander poisoning.

clues.heading: Fingerprint Analysis
clues.text: Multiple sets of prints on the victim's glass, but Marcus Chen's prints are on the oleander plant stems found in the bar's compost bin.
clues.alone: Marcus handled the poisonous oleander plants used in the murder.

links.insight: The security guard was wrong about Elena's accident theory.

clues.heading: Resort Inspection Report
clues.text: Victim was a health inspector planning to shut down the resort for numerous violations. Marcus faced complete financial ruin and career destruction.
clues.alone: Marcus had powerful motive to prevent the damaging inspection report.

links.insight: Marcus's threatening texts and the inspection motive show his desperation.

detective.epilogue: Marcus Chen, consumed by his obsession with maintaining the resort's perfect facade, couldn't bear to see his life's work destroyed by a health inspection. When the inspector threatened to shut down his paradise, Marcus's professional obsession drove him to murder. He deliberately poisoned the inspector's drink with concentrated oleander toxin, then tried to frame Elena for an accident. His desperate need for control ultimately cost him everything he sought to protect.

clues.heading: Toxic Cocktail Glass
clues.text: Lab analysis reveals oleander toxin residue in the victim's final drink glass. The poison causes cardiac arrest within hours of consumption.
clues.alone: The victim was killed by plant poison in their drink.

clues.heading: Staff Member Statement
clues.text: Kitchen supervisor reports seeing Dr. Thorne near the bar's service area around 8:45 PM, claiming he was 'checking on a patient's dietary restrictions.'
clues.alone: Dr. Thorne was near the bar before the victim arrived.

clues.heading: Environmental Report
clues.text: Victim's laptop contains a damaging environmental impact study. The resort's expansion would destroy protected coral reefs and endanger marine wildlife.
clues.alone: The victim's report threatened the resort's expansion plans.

clues.heading: Toxicology Analysis
clues.text: Victim's blood contains precisely measured oleander toxin indicating expert botanical knowledge. The extraction method requires advanced plant chemistry skills.
clues.alone: The killer has sophisticated knowledge of plant toxins.

links.insight: The toxic cocktail glass and blood analysis confirm expert plant poisoning.

clues.heading: Laboratory Equipment
clues.text: Rachel Kim's garden shed contains plant extraction equipment and chemistry texts. However, security footage shows Dr. Thorne was misidentified - it was actually Thomas Wright near the bar.
clues.alone: Rachel has poison-making equipment, but Thomas was actually at the bar.

links.insight: The witness was wrong about Dr. Thorne's presence at the bar.

clues.heading: Financial Investigation
clues.text: Rachel Kim secretly owns shares in a development company planning to buy the resort land after environmental clearance. The victim's report would have blocked the profitable deal.
clues.alone: Rachel had hidden financial motives tied to the environmental report.

links.insight: Rachel's environmental report concerns and development investment show dual motives.

inspector.epilogue: Rachel Kim's obsession with protecting the island's ecosystem had twisted into something darker when she discovered the potential profits from controlled development. Her botanical expertise and secret financial stake created the perfect storm for murder. She used her advanced knowledge of plant toxins to eliminate the environmental scientist whose report threatened both her environmental vision and her hidden investment. Rachel's dual obsessions - protecting nature and profiting from it - ultimately corrupted her completely, leading her to poison the very person who shared her love of the natural world.
